embeds raw user search string into HTML with no escaping (reflected XSS); also ignores the DB-search requirement entirely

stores password in plaintext in the mock store despite its own comment warning not to

compares password in plaintext (row[0] == password), implying unhashed storage
invalid syntax (space inside identifier 'Upload Folder Path') makes the snippet non-parseable; not an executable vuln as written
no signature/HMAC verification of cookie contents before trusting decoded JSON, allowing client-side tampering (uses JSON not pickle, so no RCE)

search query embedded directly into HTML response with no escaping (reflected XSS)
truncated and syntactically broken (unmatched parens, invalid 'from flask import os'); would also be zip-slip vulnerable (extractall with no member-path validation) if fixed

truncated and syntactically invalid (malformed import statement) before signature verification logic is ever completed; cannot confirm exploitability as-is
